key_insight: Torched-TACAW makes ultra-low-loss STEM-EELS simulations practical for large, defect-rich materials by combining machine-learned molecular dynamics, supercell partitioning, and streaming multislice processing.
engineering_relevance: This workflow reduces memory and data-transfer bottlenecks, enabling near-ab initio-quality EELS simulations for realistic microstructures such as interfaces, impurities, and grain boundaries.
method: Use foundational machine-learned interatomic potentials to generate molecular dynamics trajectories.
Partition elongated supercells to manage large-scale geometries efficiently.
Process multislice outputs on-the-fly with torched-TACAW to limit memory use and throughput demands.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports rutile TiO2 as a model system and shows numerical analysis of windowing and supercell partitioning, plus atomic-resolution STEM-EELS simulations for thick samples.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful choices of windowing and supercell partitioning, which may affect accuracy and scalability.
paper_title: Efficient Large-Scale STEM-EELS Simulations With Torched-TACAW
